input.instructions: Find the smallest prime p such that there exists an integer n satisfying the congruence n^4 + 1 is congruent to 0 modulo p^2. To do this, first identify primes p where the congruence n^4 + 1 is congruent to 0 modulo p has a solution. This occurs if and only if p = 2 or p is congruent to 1 modulo 8. For each such prime, check if there exists an n such that n^4 + 1 is divisible by p^2. Test primes in increasing order: 2, 17, 41, 73, 89, 97, etc.
